ab: adaman, mukuwa ku mudadangiya kani mulri na ma’inay.
ch: 昨天，我去部落拜訪爺爺。
en: Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

ab: sawariyan mi lra adi marepana’u, sare’ed ku kantaw.
ch: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
en: We haven’t seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

ab: mena’u i mulri kaningku, payas pakan kaningku da taramunah, di pa’au da ociya kaningku.
ch: 爺爺一看到我，馬上請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
en: As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

ab: tavu’ tu sinaleman na taramunah i mulri na ma’inay, di tatena’ da imaran.
ch: 爺爺種的西瓜很甜，真的很好吃。
en: The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

ab: sahar ku kantu ociya ni mulri na ma’inay, di imaran nu ’emau ta.
ch: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，喝起來也不錯。
en: I also like grandpa’s tea, it’s tasted good too.

ab: nu kare’alriyan, marelrang ku kani mulri na vavayan demeru da kare’a’alriyan.
ch: 中午的時候，我和奶奶一起煮午餐。
en: At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

ab: kasaduwan tu tinusiyawan ni mulri na vavayan, ulra a vulraw, ulra a vuyu, ulra a siyak di dindin diya.
ch: 奶奶煮了很豐盛的午餐，有魚、有雞、有南瓜、還有蝸牛。
en: Grandma cooked a big lunch, including fish, chicken, pumpkin, and snails.

ab: piya mi lra kare’alri mu, mukuwa mi i sayhe kikulanga.
ch: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
en: After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

ab: i mulri na vavayan melak da sadu vurasi, vu’ir di lakiw, veray kaningku, paadayan muruma’.
ch: 奶奶拿了很多地瓜、芋頭和蕗蕎給我，要我帶回家。
en: Grandma brought me a lot of sweet potatoes, taro and buckwheat, and asked me to take them home.

ab: mukuwa ku sumungara kanadu, marengaya da daduwa ku diya mena’uwa kanadu nu kadaidan lra.
ch: 我跟他們道別，說下次再來看他們。
en: I said goodbye to them and said I’d see them next time.
